operator_additions_note: Operator added 10 more to Haiku's list per V11.17.61 review: agogwe, thylacine, traverspine-gorilla, mapinguari, ebu-gogo, orang-pendek, bamboo-ape, nandi-bear, moehau, basajaun. Pattern matches Haiku's 'Extinct Species' / 'Real-Animal Sighting' buckets that Haiku undershot.

archive.name: Agogwe
archive.rationale: Operator add: small East African bipedal hominid, plausibly a misidentified bonobo / unclassified small primate. Same class as the orang-pendek borderline.

archive.name: Thylacine
archive.rationale: Operator add: real documented extinct species (Tasmanian tiger). 'Cryptid' framing is only 'did it survive extinction?' — same logic as the Megalodon / Sinomegaceros archives Haiku already flagged.

archive.name: Traverspine Gorilla
archive.rationale: Operator add: single 1913 Labrador encounter, almost certainly a bear misidentification. No corpus, no paranormal framing.

archive.name: Mapinguari
archive.rationale: Operator add: cryptozoologists explicitly frame as 'surviving giant ground sloth' — same surviving-extinct-species logic as Sinomegaceros + Megalodon already archived.

archive.name: Ebu Gogo
archive.rationale: Operator add: Flores small hominid; Homo floresiensis discovery grounds this in real paleoanthropology. Like thylacine, it's 'did a real species survive?' not paranormal.

archive.name: Orang Pendek
archive.rationale: Operator add: Sumatran small hominid; the most consensus-plausible 'undiscovered real primate' in cryptozoology. Likely a misidentified gibbon or juvenile orangutan.

archive.name: Bamboo Ape
archive.rationale: Operator add: Chinese small hominid in the orang-pendek class — plausibly an undiscovered or rare known primate.

archive.name: Nandi Bear
archive.rationale: Operator add: East African bear-like cryptid; likely a misidentified known bear, hyena, or aardvark species.

archive.name: Moehau
archive.rationale: Operator add: NZ wild-man with rare and inconsistent reports; likely an escaped pet, feral human, or folkloric exaggeration.

archive.name: Basajaun
archive.rationale: Operator add: Basque mountain wild-man; folkloric + plausibly feral human or bear, not a paranormal entity.

archive.name: Alien Big Cat
archive.rationale: Large felines in non-native regions (British Isles, Australia) are most likely escaped/feral big cats or misidentified known species; core example of real-animal drift.

archive.name: Beast of Exmoor
archive.rationale: Large black cat-like creature in English moorlands; most likely escaped/feral big cat or misidentified known species; core real-animal drift example.

archive.name: Giant Anaconda
archive.rationale: Oversized specimens of known species (anaconda) with exaggerated size claims; fits real-animal color variant/oversize individual category.

archive.name: Megaconda
archive.rationale: Oversized anaconda-like snakes with exaggerated size claims; fits real-animal color variant/oversize individual category.

archive.name: Megalodon
archive.rationale: Scientifically documented extinct shark with extensive fossil record; not a cryptid but a known extinct species.

archive.name: Mimic Tiger
archive.rationale: Striped felid or marsupial from eastern Australia; most likely misidentified known species or color variant.

archive.name: Ningen
archive.rationale: Antarctic humanoid marine cryptid from 2007 2channel forum with unverified whale research crew claims; lacks credible documentation.

archive.name: Orang Minyak
archive.rationale: Malaysian naked oil-covered humanoid with 1960s peak reports; likely folklore-based panic or hoax rather than genuine cryptid.

archive.name: Sinomegaceros
archive.rationale: Extinct giant deer genus with genetic research showing survival to 20,000 years ago; not a cryptid but a known extinct species.

archive.name: Wampus Cat
archive.rationale: Large panther-like cryptid from Appalachia; most likely escaped/feral big cat or misidentified known species.

keep.name: Adze
keep.rationale: Canonical folkloric entity from Ewe West African tradition with paranormal/supernatural framing; fits the 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Agogwe
keep.rationale: Small East African hominid with centuries of indigenous and colonial documentation; fits canonical cryptozoology despite possible real-animal explanation.

keep.name: Agropelter
keep.rationale: Appalachian apelike creature with mischievous behavior; fits regional Bigfoot variant / wild man tradition despite zero reports.

keep.name: Ahool
keep.rationale: Giant bat-like creature from Java with distinctive vocalization; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite possible oversized bat explanation.

keep.name: Almasti
keep.rationale: Eurasian Bigfoot equivalent with 19 reports and canonical cryptozoology status; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP hominid cryptids.

keep.name: Am Fear Liath Mòr
keep.rationale: Scottish mountain humanoid entity with paranormal framing; fits folkloric/regional cryptid tradition despite limited reports.

keep.name: Appalachian Wildman
keep.rationale: Large humanoid with 144 documented sightings across Appalachia; canonical regional Bigfoot variant that should be retained.

keep.name: Aswang
keep.rationale: Shape-shifting vampire-like entity from Filipino folklore with paranormal framing; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Bamboo Ape
keep.rationale: Southeast Asian bipedal primate cryptid with indigenous tradition backing; fits hominid cryptid category despite possible real undiscovered primate.

keep.name: Barghest of Yorkshire
keep.rationale: Legendary large canine from English folklore with centuries of historical accounts; fits folkloric being category with paranormal framing.

keep.name: Basajaun
keep.rationale: Forest guardian from Basque mythology with paranormal/supernatural framing; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Batsquatch
keep.rationale: Hybrid bat-humanoid from Pacific Northwest with 8 reports; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite unusual morphology.

keep.name: Beast of Busco
keep.rationale: Aquatic mystery animal (turtle-like creature); edge case rule directs to KEEP lake/aquatic cryptids even with zero reports.

keep.name: Bigfoot
keep.rationale: Canonical North American cryptid with 877 reports and anomalous paranormal framing; quintessential genuine cryptid.

keep.name: Black Shuck
keep.rationale: Ghostly black dog from East Anglian folklore with paranormal framing and 27 reports; fits folkloric being category.

keep.name: Bunyip
keep.rationale: Water-dwelling creature from Australian Aboriginal mythology; edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic mystery animals.

keep.name: Burrunjor
keep.rationale: Bipedal reptilian creature from Australian Aboriginal folklore; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite possible dinosaur misidentification.

keep.name: Cadborosaurus
keep.rationale: Sea serpent from Pacific Northwest; edge case rule directs to KEEP lake/aquatic cryptids even if 'could be a sturgeon.'

keep.name: Champ
keep.rationale: Lake Champlain serpentine creature; edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic mystery animals as core cryptozoology.

keep.name: Chupacabra
keep.rationale: Modern cryptid with 11 reports and paranormal framing; canonical cryptozoology despite possible canine misidentification.

keep.name: Dogman
keep.rationale: Bipedal wolf-like cryptid with 218 reports and anomalous paranormal framing; canonical cryptid distinct from werewolf folklore.

keep.name: Dover Demon
keep.rationale: Well-documented 1977 sighting with three independent witnesses and anomalous morphology; canonical cryptid with strong documentation.

keep.name: Dwayyo
keep.rationale: Appalachian reptilian-mammalian hybrid with 10 reports; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite unusual morphology.

keep.name: Ebu Gogo
keep.rationale: Small humanoid from Flores Island folklore with indigenous tradition; fits hominid cryptid category despite possible real undiscovered species.

keep.name: Enfield Horror
keep.rationale: Well-documented 1973 Illinois sighting wave with 14 reports and anomalous reptilian-humanoid morphology; canonical cryptid.

keep.name: Flatwoods Monster
keep.rationale: Iconic 1952 West Virginia sighting with multiple witnesses and anomalous morphology; canonical cryptid despite single report.

keep.name: Fresno Nightcrawler
keep.rationale: California cryptid with 240 reports and distinctive anomalous morphology (long legs, small upper body); canonical twentieth-century cryptid.

keep.name: Giant Spider
keep.rationale: Global cryptid legend with anomalous size (10-100 feet); anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite possible arachnid misidentification.

keep.name: Goatman
keep.rationale: Bipedal human-goat hybrid with 17 reports and paranormal framing; canonical cryptid with anomalous morphology.

keep.name: Grafton Monster
keep.rationale: Large pale humanoid from West Virginia with 9 reports; fits regional Bigfoot variant tradition.

keep.name: Grassman
keep.rationale: Ohio Bigfoot variant with 43 reports; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP regional Bigfoot variants.

keep.name: Great Lakes Sea Monster
keep.rationale: Lake serpent collection (Bessie, Tahoe Tessie); edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic mystery animals.

keep.name: Honey Island Swamp Monster
keep.rationale: Bipedal swamp creature with 3 reports and distinctive three-toed webbed footprints; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Indrid Cold
keep.rationale: Humanoid entity with telepathic contact and paranormal framing from Point Pleasant wave; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Jersey Devil
keep.rationale: Winged hooved creature with centuries of tradition and 1909 sighting wave; canonical cryptid with strong cultural status.

keep.name: Kongamato
keep.rationale: Pterosaur-like flying reptile from Zambia with indigenous documentation; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Lechuza
keep.rationale: Owl-like creature with elderly woman's face from Mexican folklore; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Lizard Man of Scape Ore Swamp
keep.rationale: Reptilian humanoid with 5 reports and well-documented 1988 encounter; canonical cryptid with distinctive morphology.

keep.name: Loch Ness Monster
keep.rationale: World's most famous lake monster with 12 reports and extensive investigation; edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic cryptids.

keep.name: Loveland Frog
keep.rationale: Frog-like humanoid with 2 reports including police officer encounters; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite later debunking claim.

keep.name: Lusca
keep.rationale: Giant octopus/shark-octopus hybrid from Bahamas; edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic mystery animals.

keep.name: Mapinguari
keep.rationale: Sloth-like creature from Amazon with indigenous tradition and scientific proposal; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status despite zero reports.

keep.name: Metoh-kangmi
keep.rationale: Himalayan bipedal humanoid with centuries of Sherpa/Tibetan tradition; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP hominid cryptids.

keep.name: Moehau
keep.rationale: New Zealand hominid cryptid with Maori oral tradition; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP hominid cryptids.

keep.name: Mokele-mbembe
keep.rationale: Long-necked semi-aquatic creature from Congo Basin with consistent indigenous accounts; edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic cryptids.

keep.name: Momo
keep.rationale: Missouri Monster with 6 reports and 1972 sighting wave; canonical regional cryptid.

keep.name: Mothman
keep.rationale: Iconic winged cryptid with 78 reports and thirteen-month sighting wave; quintessential canonical cryptid.

keep.name: Nandi Bear
keep.rationale: Large aggressive cryptid from Kenya with hyena-like morphology; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Ogopogo
keep.rationale: Okanagan Lake serpent with Syilx tradition and European documentation since 1872; edge case rule directs to KEEP aquatic cryptids.

keep.name: Orang Pendek
keep.rationale: Small Sumatran bipedal primate with footprint casts and hair samples; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP hominid cryptids.

keep.name: Orang-bati
keep.rationale: Winged humanoid from Seram Island with missionary documentation; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Ozark Howler
keep.rationale: Horned bear-sized cryptid with 8 reports and distinctive vocalization; canonical regional cryptid.

keep.name: Popobawa
keep.rationale: Shape-shifting nocturnal entity from Zanzibar/Tanzania folklore with paranormal framing; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Pukwudgie
keep.rationale: Small troll-like humanoid from Wampanoag/Lenape folklore with 9,000-year tradition; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Ropen
keep.rationale: Flying pterosaur-like creature from Papua New Guinea with bioluminescence claims; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Rougarou
keep.rationale: Werewolf-like creature from Louisiana Cajun folklore with paranormal framing; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Sheepsquatch
keep.rationale: Large white woolly cryptid from West Virginia with 5 reports and 1990s sighting surge; canonical regional cryptid.

keep.name: Skinwalker
keep.rationale: Shape-shifting entity from Navajo tradition with 188 reports and government investigation; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Skunk Ape
keep.rationale: Florida Everglades bipedal cryptid with 33 reports; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP regional Bigfoot variants.

keep.name: Spring Heeled Jack
keep.rationale: Leaping entity from Victorian England with paranormal framing and centuries of accounts; fits 'Folkloric beings' edge case category.

keep.name: Thunderbird
keep.rationale: Enormous bird with 45 reports and Native American sacred tradition; canonical cryptid with strong cultural status.

keep.name: Thylacine
keep.rationale: Officially extinct marsupial with 1,200+ post-extinction sightings and 2023 survival study; anomalous enough to warrant cryptid status.

keep.name: Traverspine Gorilla
keep.rationale: White-furred bipedal crypto-hominid from Labrador with family documentation; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP hominid cryptids.

keep.name: Urayuli
keep.rationale: Bigfoot-type cryptid from Alaska with Yup'ik tradition; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP regional Bigfoot variants.

keep.name: White Thang
keep.rationale: Ghostly white-furred cryptid from Alabama with 24 reports; canonical regional cryptid.

keep.name: Yeren
keep.rationale: Chinese reddish-haired bipedal crypto-hominid with 2,000-year tradition; edge case rule explicitly directs to KEEP hominid cryptids.

keep.name: Yowie
keep.rationale: Australian bipedal hair-covered hominid with 21 reports and Aboriginal tradition spanning millennia; canonical hominid cryptid.
